name: University of Florida/Florida Cooperative Extension - Volusia County Cooperative Extension Service
description_short: University of Florida's Family, Youth and Community Sciences department provides research-based education, extension services, and community outreach to improve individual, family, and community wellbeing.
description_long: The Family, Youth and Community Sciences (FYCS) department is part of the University of Florida's Institute of Food and Agricultural Sciences (UF/IFAS). Established as an academic and outreach unit, FYCS has evolved from its home economics roots to become an innovative research and community engagement department. The department operates as a community of scholars, professionals, and students committed to translating research into practical solutions for families and communities. The Volusia County Cooperative Extension Service operates as a local extension point for this university department's work. FYCS offers educational programming, research-based information, and extension services designed to address real-world challenges facing families, youth, and communities. Their work spans undergraduate and graduate education, academic research, and direct community outreach. The department maintains faculty and staff who work directly in communities to disseminate evidence-based knowledge. FYCS distinguishes itself through its integration of academic rigor with practical community application—research is conducted specifically to improve human conditions and is delivered through extension programming to those who need it. Programs are guided by peer-reviewed research and delivered by university-trained professionals. Unlike commercial consumer finance services, FYCS operates as a non-profit educational institution funded through public university resources, meaning services are delivered without profit motive. The honest assessment is that while FYCS provides valuable educational resources and research-based information, the website does not clearly specify consumer finance counseling as a primary service. The department focuses broadly on family, youth, and community sciences rather than specializing in credit counseling or financial capability training. Those seeking direct financial advice or credit counseling may need to contact the extension office directly to determine what specific financial education services are available.
pros: Part of University of Florida/IFAS—credible, publicly-funded academic institution with no profit motive
Research-based approach ensures information and education are grounded in evidence and scholarship
Community-focused extension model designed to bring university resources directly to local populations
Faculty and professional staff are academically trained and credentialed in their fields
Operates the Volusia County Cooperative Extension Service as a local community access point
Integrated approach addressing family systems, youth development, and community wellbeing holistically
Graduate and undergraduate programs train future professionals in family and community sciences
cons: Website does not clearly specify financial counseling, credit education, or debt management as core services
No visible mention of HUD approval, NFCC certification, or specific consumer finance credentials
Limited consumer-facing marketing makes it unclear what specific services are available to the general public
Focus appears to be primarily academic and research-based rather than direct consumer financial advisory
No pricing information, service availability hours, or clear access instructions provided on main website
best_for: Individuals seeking university-based, research-backed educational resources on family and community wellbeing
Families in Volusia County looking for local extension-based community programs and educational outreach
People preferring non-profit, publicly-funded educational resources over commercial financial services
diagnosis: FYCS is best for individuals and families seeking university-based, research-backed educational resources and extension services, particularly those in Volusia County. The main caveat is that the website does not clearly indicate whether direct consumer financial counseling or credit education is offered—interested parties should contact the Volusia County Extension Service directly to determine specific financial education availability.
services: Undergraduate degree programs in family, youth, and community sciences
Master of Science in Family, Youth and Community Sciences (on-campus and online)
Ph.D. in Youth Development and Family Sciences
Graduate Certificate in Nonprofit Leadership (on-campus and online)
Extension programming and services through Volusia County Cooperative Extension Service
Blog and educational articles on family and community topics
Community engagement and applied research initiatives
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3100 East New York Avenue, Deland, FL 32724
